title: Views - remove bottom row in wich are present the data summerized for each column
excerpt: Views - remove bottom-down row in wich are present the data summerized for each column

posts.author: Matteo.Roetto (Matteo)
posts.cookedHtml: Views - remove bottom-down row in wich are present the data summerized for each column

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Matteo.Roetto! Are you referring to the summary row I’ve outlined in red? Did you want the ability to remove this summary row?

Screen Shot 2023-02-23 at 12.30.44 pm2086×384 29.1 KB

posts.author: Matteo.Roetto (Matteo)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Bianca,
yes, exactly that, thanks. it’s possible?

Can I take advantage and ask you something else? It is possible to send, with gmail integration, emails to multiple recipients in “to” and in “cc”?

Many thanks
Matteo

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
You can actually deactivate the board footers feature via monday.labs!

Screen Shot 2023-02-24 at 10.00.46 am762×790 52.5 KB

Screen Shot 2023-02-24 at 10.10.20 am1108×200 17.3 KB

Let me know if this helps

As for your second question, you can define multiple emails in a Text Column separated by “;” and the email will be sent to each of those emails separately. You can define the values (the email addresses) of the Text column like this: example@monday.com ; example.monday@gmail.com. You just need to ensure you have chosen the text column when building the recipe:

Screen Shot 2023-02-24 at 10.11.28 am1144×554 27 KB

As an alternative, if you’d like to cc or bcc contacts, I’d instead recommend looking into Emails & Activities on monday.com which is available through our CRM product. The app allows you to also track and log all emails within one place

I hope this helps!

posts.author: Matteo.Roetto (Matteo)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Bianca,
thank you for your response.
In relation to this, I report that the feature is already disabled following your instructions (but apparently it doesn’t work):

Could you have any other suggestions?

Many thanks
Matteo

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Matteo.Roetto my sincerest apologies - can you please instead try activating this feature instead?

Screen Shot 2023-02-27 at 1.49.15 pm1120×172 16.9 KB

posts.author: amypurple (Amy Roche)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @BiancaT - I tried this, but it did not resolve it for me. Any other suggestions?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Can you try hiding the column summary for the specific columns and let me know if this works?

Screen Shot 2023-04-17 at 1.38.22 pm1016×812 64.9 KB

posts.author: amypurple (Amy Roche)
posts.cookedHtml:
It removes the summary from our data entered in Monday, but not the actual table or row. I still see this:

image001.png864×98 458 Bytes

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Ah I see what you’re referring to here - apologies I thought this was in reference to the specific data that populates that summarised row. I am afraid that this empty row isn’t currently removable at this time. I encourage you to share this request with others to increase visibility and votes

posts.author: hh513 (H)
posts.cookedHtml: Please add the ability to remove this row

posts.author: mthebeau (McKenzie Thebeau)
posts.cookedHtml: @BiancaT - I did the hide add on you mentioned here, but it still exports them with the footers. Is this intended to keep them when exported in excel?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Mckenzie!

If you deactivate this setting in monday.labs can you confirm if the footers still appear in the export?

posts.author: mthebeau (McKenzie Thebeau)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes they do still appear in the export but not in the UI screen.

posts.author: jhamb (Josh Beck)
posts.cookedHtml: The footers still appear for me regardless of how this feature is set. This would be such a helpful feature both for visual tidiness and as a time saver. Hiding the summary for each column individually can be time consuming.

posts.author: danicoss (Dani Coss)
posts.cookedHtml: This definitely needs to be an option!
